# Phase 51: Hero Components - Context

**Gathered:** 2026-04-16
**Status:** Ready for planning
**Mode:** Auto-generated (autonomous mode -- decisions derived from milestone discussion, Phase 48/49/50 deliverables, design reference, and locked architectural constraints)

<domain>
## Phase Boundary

Deliver the three "hero" components that make the swarm view feel alive for management: subagent fleet cards, the AI briefing panel, and the agent detail drawer. After this phase:

1. The swarm view shows a subagent fleet section with one glass card per agent, complete with state badge + color dot, 3 metrics (active jobs, queue depth, error count), and skill pill tags -- all fed from `swarm_agents` via the existing Phase 49 `useRealtimeTable("agents")` hook.
2. The briefing region displays a plain-English narrative generated by a dedicated **Orq.ai Briefing Agent** (not direct Anthropic/OpenAI), alongside a KPI grid with 4 cells (active jobs, human review, blocked, done today). A button lets the user regenerate the briefing on demand.
3. Briefings are cached in the `swarm_briefings` table with an `expires_at` TTL (5 minutes). A stale briefing never blocks UI render; the panel shows the cached copy with an "updated X min ago" footer and refreshes automatically every 30 min via an Inngest cron.
4. Clicking a fleet card opens a slide-out drawer showing the agent's detail view: active count, average cycle time, behavior description, recent communication timeline (last 5 events grouped by `trace_id` from `agent_events`), and a local workflow tag row.

**Out of scope (later phases):**
- Kanban board + terminal stream (Phase 52)
- Delegation graph + Gantt swimlanes (Phase 53)
- Smart filter buttons NAV-04 (Phase 52)
- Recursive agent detail view with mini sub-graph inside the drawer (V8+)
- Migrating existing legacy pages to V7 tokens (Phase 54)
- Editing briefing prompts in-UI (not required; iteration is via Orq.ai studio)

</domain>

<decisions>
## Implementation Decisions

### Fleet Cards (FLEET-01..04)
- **D-01:** Fleet cards live in `web/components/v7/fleet/subagent-fleet-card.tsx` and render inside a wrapper `web/components/v7/fleet/subagent-fleet.tsx` that consumes `useRealtimeTable("agents")`. The wrapper renders an empty-state when `agents.length === 0` instead of the placeholder grid.
- **D-02:** Card schema fields: `agent_name`, `role`, `status`, `metrics.active_jobs`, `metrics.queue_depth`, `metrics.error_count`, `skills: string[]`. Metrics and skills are JSONB on the row, so we cast via a local Zod schema (`fleetAgentMetricsSchema`) with safe fallbacks to `0` / `[]` to survive schema drift.
- **D-03:** State badge maps `SwarmAgentStatus` -> color + copy:
  | status | dot color token | badge copy |
  |---|---|---|
  | `active` | `--v7-teal` | "Running" |
  | `idle` | `--v7-muted` | "Idle" |
  | `waiting` | `--v7-amber` | "Waiting" |
  | `error` | `--v7-red` | "Error" |
  | `offline` | `--v7-faint` | "Offline" |
- **D-04:** Click-to-open-drawer uses a client-side store (`web/lib/v7/use-drawer-store.ts`, tiny Zustand-free useState lifted via React Context) rather than URL state. Rationale: drawer is ephemeral, navigation should not change the URL (drawer ≠ page), and Phase 49 already established a swarm-view-scoped context pattern with the Realtime provider. A second "DrawerContext" keeps the same boundary.
- **D-05:** Cards use `<GlassCard>` from Phase 48 as the outer surface. Hover lift (`translateY(-3px)`) + border accent matches the design reference.

### Briefing Panel (BRIEF-01..03)
- **D-06:** Two files ship: `web/components/v7/briefing/briefing-panel.tsx` (client component, reads `useRealtimeTable("briefings")`) and `web/components/v7/briefing/kpi-grid.tsx` (client subset). Panel owns layout of narrative + KPI grid inside a single `<GlassCard>`.
- **D-07:** Narrative generator: a **dedicated Orq.ai Briefing Agent** deployed under the key `swarm-briefing-agent` (created in Plan 51-02 via `mcp__orqai-mcp__create_agent`). Prompt is XML-tagged, uses `response_format: json_schema`, validated on our end with Zod (`briefingOutputSchema`). Primary model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`; fallbacks: `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5`.
- **D-08:** Generation path: a Next.js **server action** `web/lib/v7/briefing/actions.ts#generateBriefing(swarmId)` runs server-side, calls the Briefing Agent via MCP (`mcp__orqai-mcp__get_agent` + a `deploy`-style invocation) using the shared `callOrqaiMcp` helper from Phase 50 pointed at the Orq.ai Agents API (`/v2/agents/{agent_id}/invoke` equivalent via MCP), Zod-validates the output, and writes a new `swarm_briefings` row with `expires_at = now() + 5 min`. Realtime picks up the INSERT and the panel re-renders.
- **D-09:** Cache TTL: **5 minutes**. The server action short-circuits if a briefing for this swarm exists with `expires_at > now()`. The 30-min BRIEF-02 Inngest cadence is the outer refresh heartbeat; the 5-min TTL is the freshness floor for on-demand regenerations.
- **D-10:** Briefing input: recent `agent_events` (last 30 min) grouped by event_type + counts, swarm_agents roster with statuses, aggregate swarm_jobs counts by stage. Fetched server-side in `briefing/actions.ts` via `createAdminClient` (service role, bypasses RLS).
- **D-11:** Output schema:
  ```ts
  {
    headline: string,    // <= 90 chars, declarative, plain-English
    summary: string,     // 2-4 sentences on health + bottlenecks
    alerts: Array<{ severity: "info"|"warn"|"critical", message: string }>,
    suggested_actions: Array<{ action: string, rationale: string }>
  }
  ```
- **D-12:** On Orq.ai call failure, the server action swallows the error, logs to `settings.orqai_briefing_last_error`, and returns the last cached briefing (even if expired). **UI never blocks on generation.** If no cached briefing exists at all, render a subdued "Briefing will be available after the first run" placeholder.
- **D-13:** On-demand refresh is a form action inside the panel header, calling `generateBriefing(swarmId)` from a `<form action={...}>` so the server action pattern is idiomatic Next.js 16. Loading state is tracked with `useTransition`.
- **D-14:** Inngest 30-min cron lives in `web/lib/inngest/functions/briefing-refresh.ts`. Iterates every `projects` row with at least one `swarm_agents` entry and calls the same `generateBriefing` logic. Registered alongside `orqai-trace-sync` in `api/inngest/route.ts`.

### Agent Detail Drawer (DRAW-01..04)
- **D-15:** Drawer component: `web/components/v7/drawer/agent-detail-drawer.tsx`. Uses the existing shadcn **Sheet** (`web/components/ui/sheet.tsx`) as the Radix primitive, themed with V7 tokens. Rationale: drawer and sheet are the same pattern; we avoid adding a new dependency. The sheet's `side="right"` + a custom V7 glass-bg override gets us the target look.
- **D-16:** Drawer receives `agent` prop from the parent shell based on the currently-open drawer ID in `DrawerContext`. Open/close is controlled by context.
- **D-17:** Communication timeline: **last 5 agent_events rows where `agent_name === drawer.agent.agent_name`**, sorted by `created_at` desc. Grouped visually by `trace_id` -- events in the same trace render under a shared trace-id eyebrow label (truncated to first 8 chars). The timeline re-renders live because it reads `useRealtimeTable("events")` -- no extra subscription needed.
- **D-18:** Mini hierarchy description (DRAW-02) is derived from `SwarmAgent.role` + a templated sentence: `"${agent_name} behaves like a micro-orchestrator for ${role}: intake, run, escalate or delegate."`. When `role` is null we render a terse fallback. This is deterministic, not LLM-generated -- consistent with the design reference's static `drawerDesc`.
- **D-19:** Average cycle time: computed from `agent_events` pairs matching `event_type IN ('thinking','done')` by `span_id` -- duration is `ended_at - started_at`. Derived client-side from the same realtime events array; bounded to the last 20 completed spans for the agent.
- **D-20:** Workflow stages row (DRAW-04) is a static tag row: `["Intake", "Run", "Verify", "Escalate", "Done"]` -- these are the canonical agent lifecycle stages from the design reference. Dynamic per-agent workflows are out of scope for V7.0 and deferred to V8.

### Layout Wiring
- **D-21:** Replace the placeholder regions in `web/components/v7/swarm-layout-shell.tsx` with the real components:
  - Briefing placeholder -> `<BriefingPanel swarmId={swarmId} />`
  - KPI placeholder -> collapsed (KPI grid now lives INSIDE `BriefingPanel`) -- the second column becomes a `<MiniDelegationGraphPlaceholder />` (still a placeholder until Phase 53).
  - Subagent fleet placeholder -> `<SubagentFleet />`
  - Kanban/Terminal placeholders remain (Phase 52).
- **D-22:** The shell also mounts a `<DrawerProvider>` + `<AgentDetailDrawer />` sibling at the root so any fleet card can trigger it regardless of nesting depth.

### Test Data Seeding
- **D-23:** Since `agent_events` and `swarm_agents` are empty (Inngest cron not deployed), we ship a **fixture SQL file** at `supabase/fixtures/51-test-data.sql` that seeds:
  - 3 `swarm_agents` rows on swarm `f8df0bce-ed24-4b77-b921-7fce44cabbbb` (EASY Email Agent Swarm): one `active`, one `idle`, one `error`.
  - 12 `agent_events` rows spread across 3 `trace_id`s, mixed event types (thinking/done/tool_call/tool_result), distinct span_ids, created_at staggered in the last 15 minutes.
- **D-24:** Fixture file is NOT auto-applied; execution is manual via Supabase Studio OR a one-off curl to the Management API (documented in the file header). This keeps the fixture idempotent (UPSERT on `(id)`) and safe to re-run.

### Folded Todos
None -- no relevant todos surfaced.

### Claude's Discretion
- Internal file organization within `web/components/v7/{fleet,briefing,drawer}/`
- Loading skeleton appearance (GlassCard with shimmer vs. empty)
- Zustand-free store pattern (useState + Context) for drawer open state vs. useReducer
- Whether to memoize metrics derivation (depends on swarm size; start unmemoized)
- Exact icon choices from lucide-react per state badge

</decisions>

- Prompt patterns: `<role>`, `<task>`, `<context>`, `<constraints>`, `<output_format>`

<code_context>
## Existing Code Insights

### Reusable Assets
- `GlassCard` (Phase 48) -- base for every panel, card, drawer surface
- `useRealtimeTable` (Phase 49) -- the ONLY way Phase 51 reads Supabase; no new channels
- `callOrqaiMcp` (Phase 50) -- the ONLY way Phase 51 calls Orq.ai; enforces 45s timeout + bearer auth
- `createAdminClient` (server) -- for service-role writes from server actions (briefing INSERT)
- shadcn Sheet -- drawer primitive

### Established Patterns
- Server component shells + `"use client"` leaves at the smallest boundary
- Zod validation at every LLM output boundary (`.safeParse`, fallback on failure)
- Token-driven styling via `--v7-*` variables, never hex literals in components
- Realtime via context + `useRealtimeTable(table)`, never per-component channels
- Inngest crons: every side effect inside `step.run()`; registered in `api/inngest/route.ts`

### Integration Points
- `web/components/v7/swarm-layout-shell.tsx` -- modify: swap 3 placeholders for real components
- `web/lib/v7/` -- add: `briefing/actions.ts`, `drawer/drawer-context.tsx`, `briefing/schema.ts`
- `web/lib/inngest/functions/briefing-refresh.ts` -- new cron
- `web/app/api/inngest/route.ts` -- register new function in `serve()` array
- `supabase/fixtures/51-test-data.sql` -- test seed for verification

</code_context>

<specifics>
## Specific Ideas

- Fleet card header layout (referencing design): `{agent_name} ... [state badge]` with dot, role subtitle below
- State badge is a pill, 7px 11px padding, border + `rgba(255,255,255,0.04)` bg, dot 8px
- Card metrics grid: `grid-cols-3` with tiny labels on top (`--v7-faint`, 12px uppercase with letter-spacing 0.1em)
- Skill row: `flex-wrap gap-2`, each pill is `rounded-[var(--v7-radius-pill)]` + `--v7-muted` text
- Briefing headline: Cabinet Grotesk, 1.8rem (28.8px), max-width 14ch
- KPI grid: 4 columns @ `lg`, 2 columns @ `md`, 1 column @ `sm`
- Briefing top eyebrow: `[pulse-dot] Autonomous briefing` -- pulse dot uses `--v7-teal` with CSS animation
- Drawer width: `min(540px, calc(100vw - 20px))`
- Drawer timeline event bullet: 12px colored circle with `box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px color-mix(...)` halo
- Trace-id grouping header: `<small>trace {id.slice(0,8)}</small>` in `--v7-faint`
- Briefing panel eyebrow copy: `Autonomous briefing`
- Empty fleet copy: `No subagents registered. Deploy agents via Orq.ai to see them here.`
- Empty briefing copy: `Briefing will appear once the first agents report in.`
- Refresh button copy: `Regenerate` (icon: `RefreshCw` from lucide)
- "Updated N min ago" uses `date-fns` `formatDistanceToNow`

</specifics>
